rationale: The cited paper is used directly in the methods core, not just as background. The citing paper states that, in line with Dong, Manchanda, and Chintagunta (2009), it estimates a structural competitive-reaction model for detailing allocation and interprets outcomes via marginal costs and benefits under equilibrium assumptions. It also follows that paper in a robustness specification and uses it as an empirical benchmark for plausible detailing-cost magnitudes. These uses show meaningful methodological borrowing that supports the citing paper's analysis.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Dong et al. (2009)'s strategic detailing modeling approach as a key methodological component of its own framework. It embeds that approach into a new data-enrichment setup (revealed plus stated competitive-reaction data) to predict consequences of unprecedented sales-force policy changes.
